sections.paragraph:
κάρφος, ους, τό (Aeschyl., Hdt. et al.; Gen 8:11) a small piece of straw, chaff, or wood, to denote someth. quite insignificant, speck, splinter, chip (cp. the proverb κινεῖν μηδὲ κ. Aristoph., Lysias 474 and Herodas 1, 54; 3, 67.—Ion of Chios [V b.c.]: 392 Fgm. 6 p. 280, 7 Jac. of a tiny foreign object in a wine cup. Grave ins, Kaibel 980, 9 [ὁ εὐσεβὴς] οὐδὲ κάρφος ἐβλάβη) Mt 7:3ff; Lk 6:41f; Ox 1 verso, 2 (ASyn. 68, 44)=GTh 26.—For lit. s. on δοκός.—DELG s.v. κάρφω. M-M.

Aeschyl Aeschyl , V b.c.—List 5

Hdt. Hdt. = Herodotus, V b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

someth. someth. = something

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

Aristoph Aristoph , V–IV b.c.—List 5

Lysias Lysias , V–IV b.c.—List 5

Herodas Herodas , a.k.a. Herondas, III b.c.—List 5

Ion Ion of Chios , V b.c.—List 5

Fgm. Fgm. = fragment, fragmentary

Jac. Jac. = Die Fragmente der griechischen Historiker, ed. FJacoby—Lists 5, 6

ins Ins, ins = Inscription, Inschrift, inscription(s). Without a period, esp. in lists, as at the beginning of entries; the capitalized form is used in titles. In conjunction with literary works this abbr. refers to the title or description of contents.

Kaibel Kaibel = Epigrammata Graeca ex Lapidibus Conlecta, ed. GK.—List 3

Ox Ox = POxy, but refers to fragments of documents esp. relating to the lexical data base—List 1

ASyn. ASyn. = KAland, Synopsis Quattuor Evangeliorum13—Lists 1, 6

GTh GTh = Gospel of Thomas in Coptic or in Oxy. documents—Lists 1

lit. lit. = literal(ly); literature (refererences to [scholarly] literature)

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 510
